message:
fix(core): close symlink-based path traversal bypass for non-existent target files

The prior path traversal guards in config_profiles and rulesets only
validated paths that already existed on disk, because they called
canonicalize() on the resolved path directly. An attacker could bypass
the check by requesting a path like "symlink_to_outside/new.toml" where
the final component does not yet exist — canonicalize() would fail on
the non-existent file, and the guard would be skipped entirely.

Fix by walking up the ancestor chain to find the nearest existing path
component, then canonicalizing that ancestor and checking whether it
falls within the managed directory. This catches symlink escapes even
when the leaf file has not been created yet.

Specific changes:
- config_profiles.rs: resolve_managed_profile_path() now finds the
  nearest existing ancestor via resolved.ancestors().find(|a| a.exists())
  and validates that ancestor's canonical path stays within profiles_dir.
  Adds test: resolve_profile_path_rejects_symlink_parent_escape_for_new_file
- rulesets.rs: Identical fix in resolve_managed_ruleset_path() with
  corresponding test: load_profile_by_name_rejects_symlink_parent_escape_for_new_file
- extensions.rs: Add ensure_managed_extension_path() call to the direct
  file lookup path in remove_extension(), closing a gap where "../secret"
  could delete files outside the extensions directory. Adds test:
  remove_extension_rejects_parent_escape_from_direct_lookup
- sessionhandler.rs: Replace .len() > 0 with !.is_empty() (clippy)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
